stories.headline: April crypto hacks blow past $606M in 18 days as Lazarus Group bags two of three biggest hauls
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols lost $606M across 12 incidents in the first 18 days of April, the worst month since February 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Two state-backed thefts wiped out a year of DeFi security progress and are reshaping how lenders and restakers think about key management.
the_details: KelpDAO drained for ~$292M on April 18, surpassing Drift's $285M February exploit as the largest hack of 2026.
North Korea's Lazarus Group is attributed to both KelpDAO and Drift after months of social-engineering operator keys.
Private-key compromises now drive the bulk of 2026 losses, shifting the threat from smart-contract code to human ops.

stories.headline: Bitcoin slams into $80K wall, retreats below $78K despite billions in fresh stablecoin liquidity
stories.tldr: BTC briefly punched above $79,000 in Asian hours before sellers concentrated around $80K dragged price back below $78,000.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Onchain stablecoin deposits and ETF demand are loaded for upside, but $80K is now the line that decides whether April closes as the best month in a year.
the_details: BTC trades near $79,123 on 2.04% daily gain with $24.7B volume; total crypto market cap reclaims $2.71T.
Roughly $5B in fresh USDT minted in April fuels speculation that sidelined capital is ready to bid above resistance.
Fear & Greed Index jumps to 47 (Neutral) from 33 (Fear) yesterday as Iran peace-talk headlines lift risk.

stories.headline: BitMine ETH stack tops 5 million after 101,901 ETH buy, the largest treasury grab since December
stories.tldr: BitMine added 101,901 ETH last week, pushing its treasury above 5 million ETH and cementing it as the largest corporate ETH holder.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A single buyer is absorbing supply at a pace that rivals ETF flows, tightening the float just as ETH lags BTC's rally.
the_details: Purchase is BitMine's biggest single accumulation since December 2025, executed as ETH traded near $2,320.
Treasury now holds over 5,000,000 ETH worth roughly $11.6B at current spot prices.
ETH is up 2.2% on the session but remains a structural laggard versus Bitcoin in 2026 to date.

stories.headline: SEC carves out broker-registration exemption for decentralized trading interfaces in April 13 staff letter
stories.tldr: SEC Trading and Markets staff said it won't object to 'Covered User Interface Providers' offering crypto-asset-security trading without broker-dealer registration.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The carve-out clears a major legal overhang for frontends like Uniswap and could unlock a wave of US-domiciled DeFi launches in Q2.
the_details: Staff statement issued April 13 covers software interfaces that prepare and submit transactions in crypto-asset securities.
Pairs with the March 17 SEC-CFTC joint release establishing a five-category token taxonomy across agencies.
Coalition of 100+ firms including Coinbase and Ripple now pressing Senate Banking to mark up the Clarity Act.

stories.headline: Aave lands on Solana via Sunrise DeFi, tapping a $5.6B onchain market and $15.35B stablecoin pool
stories.tldr: Aave is bridging Ethereum-backed liquidity to Solana through Sunrise DeFi to chase faster execution and lower fees.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The biggest EVM lender stepping onto SVM is the clearest signal yet that liquidity, not ideology, decides where DeFi ships next.
the_details: Solana's $5.6B DeFi base and $15.35B stablecoin pool give Aave instant scale outside the EVM stack.
Move follows Solana's Alpenglow upgrade slashing finality below 200ms and Firedancer's path to 1M TPS.
Spot SOL ETF inflows have collapsed 92% from $419M in November 2025 to $34M in April, raising stakes for native demand.
